issues.description:
[bug-hunt] Logging streak is ALWAYS 0 - date format mismatch between backend history and frontend streak calc (streak chip + success-message streak never render).

Expected: after logging weight on consecutive days, /log-weight shows the "N-day logging streak" chip and the post-save message appends "N-day streak".
Actual: streak is permanently 0 for every user. Backend /api/v1/weight/log-data returns daily_weight_history[].date as strftime('%b %d') e.g. "Jul 02" (nyu_backend/backend.py:6061,6072), but the frontend streak memo builds ISO strings "2026-07-02" and checks days.has(iso) (src/pages/LogWeight.tsx:316-329). No ISO string ever matches "Jul 02", so the loop breaks immediately -> streak 0 -> the chip at LogWeight.tsx:524 and the streak suffix at :468 are dead code in production.

Repro (code-level, deterministic): log weight today and yesterday, open /log-weight -> no streak chip despite 2-day run. Verify via console: fetch /weight/log-data and compare date format vs the memo's template string.

Secondary defect same feature: backend carry-forwards the previous weight into unlogged days (backend.py:6064-6074), so even with matching formats the streak would count days the user never logged. Fix should compare on ISO dates AND only count real log days (or compute streak server-side).
issues.author: shakeebshaan

fix.description: Root cause: backend daily_weight_history dates were 'Jul 02' display format; frontend streak memo built ISO strings that never matched. Fix: backend now emits date_iso in ISO format (backend.py:6064,6077); frontend streak uses p.date_iso ?? p.date and filters p.logged!==false to exclude carry-forward days. Streak now counts real consecutive log days correctly.

judge.note: Root cause correctly identified and fixed at both layers. date_iso field confirmed in backend diff (d7ab770). Frontend streak loop at LogWeight.tsx:318-336 uses ISO keys and filters logged!==false. Both carry-forward and date-format defects addressed. Complete fix.

previousFix.description: Root cause: backend strftime('%b %d') dates ('Jul 02') never matched frontend ISO strings ('2026-07-02'), so streak Set lookup always missed + carry-forward days falsely counted. Fix: backend daily_weight_history now includes date_iso (ISO-8601) and logged:bool; frontend streak memo uses date_iso and filters logged:false carry-forwards. Scoped tests: 91/91 pass.

judge.note: Root cause is deterministic (date format mismatch), fix is surgical (add date_iso+logged fields, update memo filter). 91 scoped unit tests green. Backend deployed + migration applied. Both secondary defects (format mismatch + carry-forward inflation) fully addressed.

issues.author: shakeebshaan

fix.description: Complete end-to-end onboarding walk captured: 36 screens from intro carousel through workout builder (loading animation × 8 frames), diet builder (loading animation × 8 frames), plan overview/tour, to dashboard. Always-custom condensed flow verified end-to-end. Report (36 screens) attached inline — click the report button to view all captures.

judge.note: All phases covered: intro carousel, You/Goal (Basics/Body/Goal/Why tabs), Training intensity, Health & Food + dietary style, Workout builder full AI build with loading states, Diet builder full AI build with loading states, Plan overview, Dashboard. Every screen the owner asked for is in the inline report. Capture script also hardened: MealPrefsSheet skip-and-build handler added.

issues.reviewReply: Do a complete end to end onboarding of a user if you have a user whose onboarding is already completed then delete the data and do the onboarding as fresh and capture every single screenshot
issues.reportLabel: Fresh onboarding walk — 36 screens intro to dashboard

issues.author: shakeebshaan
issues.reviewReason: Owner-only action: swapping the Razorpay API key requires access to the production .env file (RAZORPAY_KEY_ID on the server). The app currently shows 'test' mode correctly. Once you update the .env on the prod server with the live key and restart nyu-backend, payments will go live. Steps: SSH to prod, edit ~/nyu_backend/.env: RAZORPAY_KEY_ID=rzp_live_xxx RAZORPAY_KEY_SECRET=xxx, then sudo systemctl restart nyu-backend.

fix.description: Goal/gender/activity breakdowns now render as formatted pill chips ('fat loss 82 · recomp 14 · muscle gain 13'). Any 'key:count key:count' metric string auto-converts to .m-chips — committed in qa-issues f47ab70.

judge.note: Screenshot shows ANALYTICS & SEGMENTATION section with 3 breakdown metrics now rendered as pill chips instead of raw colon-separated strings. Fix is live on GitHub Pages.

fix.description: Day-over-day delta chips added to all numeric hero metrics and rows. Hero cards show ▲4 (Total users), ▲4 (Monthly active), ▲2 (Active subs). Row deltas compare vs the previous day's snapshot via metrics-history.json in private repo (now has 5d history). Committed in qa-issues f47ab70.

judge.note: Screenshot shows Metrics tab hero cards with green triangle delta chips (▲4, ▲4, ▲2) showing points up from yesterday. 5-day history stored in private repo. Fix live on GitHub Pages.

fix.description: Root cause: MealPrefsSheet always showed all 3 cooking-style questions even if the user already answered them in a prior session. Fix: pendingQuestions filters CHIP_QUESTIONS to those not already answered in initialPrefs; if all are answered, onDone is called immediately (no sheet shown). Users who already answered cooking style / cook time / spice level skip straight to the build.

judge.note: MealPrefsSheet.tsx:81-91 pendingQuestions useMemo filters by initialPrefs; nothingToAsk=true calls onDone immediately. Questions already in saved draft are skipped.

fix.description: Root cause: TheMealDB keyword fallback (compound AI names like 'Chicken Roti Roll' miss the full-name search, so it searches 'chicken') always took the FIRST result - every chicken-named meal in a plan got the identical photo. Fix: offset into the result list by a crc32 hash of the full meal name, so different dishes spread across results while the same name stays deterministic. Backend 067c5ef, deployed; evidence card shows 4 same-keyword meals now resolving 4 distinct photos from the live prod code path.

judge.note: Live prod output verified: 4 same-keyword meal names -> 4 distinct TheMealDB photos; existing rows regenerate via the image worker on next enqueue

fix.description: Root cause: MealSlotCard showed all ingredient names inline as text in every coach-built card, turning a 7-ingredient meal into a wall of text. Fix: coach meals now render a compact 'N ingredients | View & adjust' tap target; the full ingredient list + editor lives behind the Adjust Sheet, keeping the card clean. Manual meals retain the inline editor.

judge.note: MealSlotCard.tsx:390 compactBody renders count + View&adjust tap; isCoachCard gate at :596. Full editorBody in Adjust Sheet at :707. Ingredient wall-of-text gone from card view.

fix.description: Root cause: plan cards had no per-card checkout action; only the page-bottom CTA triggered Razorpay. Fix: each plan card's 'Subscribe — ₹X' button calls handleRazorpayPayment(planId) directly, starting Razorpay checkout for that plan immediately on tap.

judge.note: ChoosePlan.tsx:645 per-card Subscribe wired with planIdOverride. Screenshot shows 'Subscribe - 1299' CTA inline on the plan card. Razorpay script preloaded at mount.

issues.author: shakeebshaan
issues.reviewReason: Design decision flagged for owner input. 3-screen sequence: (1) Your plan overview [review before build] (2) Coach building your plan [loading screen] (3) Your plan is ready [post-build]. Recommendation: MERGE screen 1 into screen 3. Remove the pre-build overview (it duplicates what the plan-ready screen shows) and keep only (2) building + (3) plan ready. This saves the user 1 screen and reduces redundancy without losing the building-animation moment or the final summary. If owner agrees, will implement by removing/simplifying PlanReadyOverview and adding the key data points to PostPlanTour. Or keep all 3 if the pre-build review has retention value.

fix.description: Root cause: ScrollableRuler (and ScrollableScale) positioned ticks via paddingLeft, but tick absolute-left ignored that padding; at scrollLeft=0 the center line hit ~halfW into the visible ticks, making the minimum ~halfW/tickSpacing steps unreachable. Fix: ticks rendered at halfW+i*tickSpacing from the div edge (no padding); scroll container width = totalWidth + containerWidth so min is reachable at scrollLeft=0.

judge.note: ScrollableRuler.tsx:235 style left:halfW+i*tickSpacing. paddingLeft/Right removed. visibleTick range adjusted for halfW offset. Same fix applied to ScrollableScale. The height slider minimum is now reachable.

history.note: find all such things from the entire app

previousFix.description: Root cause: ChoosePlan X button used navigate(-1); after fresh signup the history entry before /choose-plan is the OTP/verify screen so 'back' landed there. Fix: X now navigates to '/dashboard' with replace:true — always a valid exit regardless of history depth.

judge.note: ChoosePlan.tsx:840 navigate('/dashboard',{replace:true}). Screenshot shows X button top-left on choose-plan page. Simple one-line fix addressing the root cause.
